authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards, 2 CFR Part 200
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Government-wide primary regulation for federal award administration, documentation, prior approvals, records retention, access, closeout, and audit evidence for federally sponsored research.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sponsored-project administration, prior-approval gating, reporting, retention, access, closeout, and audit-readiness checks

authoritative_sources.title: NIH Grants Policy Statement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NIH policy source for recipient responsibilities, terms and conditions, prior approvals, reporting, closeout, and records for NIH-funded research administration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: NIH proposal, award, recipient responsibility, reporting, prior approval, and closeout workflow handling
used_for: recipient responsibility and eRA Commons evidence dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: Proposal & Award Policies & Procedures Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NSF guide for proposal preparation, submission, award administration, reporting, responsible conduct, and approval boundaries for NSF-sponsored research.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: NSF proposal submission, authorized organizational representative routing, award administration, reporting, and responsible-conduct dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: Protection of Human Subjects, 45 CFR Part 46
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary Common Rule regulation for human-subjects protections, IRB review, informed consent, records, and study-protection boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Protocol, IRB status, human-subjects records, consent, continuing review, and ethics escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Informed Consent FAQs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OHRP guidance for informed-consent handling, supporting refusal or escalation when consent status changes study administration or participant-data handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Consent artifact validation, participant-data access gating, and consent-status escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Promoting Objectivity in Research, 42 CFR Part 50 Subpart F
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary PHS regulation governing investigator financial-conflict disclosures, institutional review, management, reporting, and recordkeeping for PHS-funded research.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: FCOI disclosure, management-plan evidence, sponsor reporting, and conflict-record escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act Regulations, 34 CFR Part 99
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for education-record access, inspection, consent, disclosure, and privacy boundaries when research administration uses learner or assessment records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Learner-record privacy, consent gating, access control, and disclosure escalation

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA and Student Privacy Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education Student Privacy Policy Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Department of Education privacy guidance translating FERPA into operational education-record access, disclosure, consent, and school-official handling expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Operational privacy handling and minimum-necessary disclosure posture

authoritative_sources.title: Institutional Records and Examinations, 34 CFR 668.24
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Title IV regulation defining institutional record maintenance and examination obligations relevant when research workflows use enrollment, learner, program, or financial-aid-linked records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Institutional education-record maintenance, retention, and examination-readiness checks

authoritative_sources.title: Common Education Data Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official education data standards initiative supporting consistent learner, course, program, organization, assessment, and credential terminology across higher-education systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system learner, program, faculty, assessment, and system terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Overview of Accreditation in the United States
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Department of Education source describing higher-education accreditation roles and quality-assurance boundaries relevant when research evidence supports program or institutional review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accreditation-adjacent evidence handling, owner routing, and quality-authority boundary control

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify whether the request is administrative maintenance, evidence packaging, research or lab workflow status, owner tracking, or an out-of-scope protocol, ethics, grant, finance, learner-record, accreditation, safety, privacy, or legal decision.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Protocol, award, learner, and laboratory context collection
workflow_stages.description: Collect sponsor, PI, proposal or award identifier, protocol number, IRB status supplied by the authorized office, consent context, project and budget period, learner or faculty dependencies, schedule context, and tenant source hierarchy before using records.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Document, evidence, and system-of-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether proposal packages, award files, sponsor terms, prior approvals, IRB records, consent materials, FCOI evidence, study files, assessment evidence, laboratory schedule records, and closeout materials are present, current, linked, and owner-mapped.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and status packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare record update notes, research or lab workflow summaries, protocol evidence packets, administrative status notes, due-date trackers, and audit summaries without deciding protocol approval, IRB status, consent validity, cost allowability, or academic outcome.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, consent, sponsor, and publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Check FERPA, Common Rule, OHRP guidance, sponsor terms, federal award retention rules, tenant privacy policy, data-use constraints, retention schedules, access permissions, and audit logs before disclosure, deletion, publication, transfer, or reclassification.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Formal approval and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing approvals, conflicting official records, IRB determinations, consent validity, sponsor prior approvals, grant allowability, FCOI management adequacy, budget approval, lab safety, registrar decisions, grading, instructional judgment, accreditation signoff, privacy decisions, and legal interpretation.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

artifacts: Principal investigator, department, sponsor, funding opportunity, proposal identifier, award number, project period, budget period, sponsor terms, reporting calendar, and closeout checklist

Proposal routing record, authorized organizational representative status, budget or cost-share references, sponsor prior approval, no-cost extension request, subaward records, and grant correspondence

IRB protocol number, review type, approval or exemption status supplied by the authorized office, amendment status, continuing review dates, consent materials, recruitment materials, participant population, and data-use constraints
PHS FCOI disclosure, conflict review status, management-plan reference, institutional reporting evidence, investigator training references, and sponsor or institutional official owner notes
Learner roster, faculty appointment or effort reference, SIS or LMS links, assessment evidence, schedule documents, program or accreditation context, and official education-record access basis

Research administration system links, IRB system links, sponsor portal links, grants administration records, finance or ERP references, document repository records, source-system timestamps, access logs, and exception summaries
Laboratory or study schedule, facility or equipment dependency, biosafety or lab safety routing flag, specimen or data transfer constraints, and owner due-date map
systems_of_record: Research administration and proposal routing systems
Sponsor portals such as eRA Commons, Research.gov, and agency-specific reporting systems
Finance, ERP, effort reporting, procurement, and subaward systems
Faculty appointment, HR, and effort certification systems
Scheduling, laboratory, facility, and equipment reservation systems
Assessment, accreditation evidence, and program review repositories
Records management, retention, access-control, and document management repositories
Governance, committee, ticketing, and owner-tracking systems

allowed: Classify research administration requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.

Validate whether protocol, grant, sponsor, consent, learner, faculty, schedule, laboratory, assessment, accreditation, and research administration records are present, current, source-linked, owner-mapped, and ready for administrative use.

Prepare record update notes, document completeness summaries, research or lab workflow summaries, administrative status notes, protocol evidence packets, retention/access escalations, and audit-trail summaries.

Recommend administrative next steps, owner routing, and due-date follow-up without claiming IRB, ethics, consent, sponsor, grant, finance, safety, registrar, grading, instructional, accreditation, legal, privacy, or official approval authority.
escalate: Required tenant policies, sponsor terms, source hierarchy, official records, system retrieval hooks, consent basis, retention rules, or authority maps are missing.
Research administration, IRB, sponsor portal, finance, SIS, LMS, document repository, public authority, or tenant policy records materially conflict.

The answer depends on protocol approval, IRB status, exempt determination, continuing review, consent validity, participant rights, grant allowability, sponsor prior approval, budget approval, FCOI management adequacy, biosafety, lab safety, accreditation finding, grade change, instructional judgment, official registrar decision, privacy decision, or legal interpretation.

Disclosure, deletion, publication, transfer, retention, access handling, or data reclassification cannot be resolved from FERPA, consent, Common Rule, sponsor terms, retention schedule, data-use restriction, or tenant policy.
refuse: Request asks the lane to approve protocols, determine IRB status, validate consent, decide participant eligibility, certify grant compliance, approve budgets, determine cost allowability, approve prior approvals, certify FCOI management, change official learner records, approve accreditation findings, grade work, or make an instructional or ethics judgment.

Request asks the lane to impersonate principal investigator, instructor, reviewer, registrar, IRB, research compliance officer, sponsor grants officer, institutional official, privacy officer, legal counsel, financial approver, biosafety officer, lab safety officer, or accreditation reviewer.
Required records or authoritative context are missing and would change the outcome.

Stakeholder asks to alter official protocol, IRB, grant, award, sponsor, finance, learner, schedule, accreditation, or study records without documented authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and auditable change trail.

domain_failure_modes: Treating administrative completeness as IRB approval, consent validity, protocol acceptability, participant eligibility, sponsor acceptance, cost allowability, budget approval, FCOI compliance, accreditation compliance, grade correctness, instructional adequacy, or legal conclusion.

Changing research administration, IRB, sponsor, finance, SIS, LMS, protocol, grant, award, laboratory schedule, accreditation, or study records without delegated update authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and audit trail.

Proceeding when sponsor terms, award number, protocol number, IRB status, consent materials, project period, reporting due date, closeout status, learner-record basis, approval owner, or source hierarchy is missing or conflicting.

Disclosing, deleting, publishing, transferring, or reclassifying learner, faculty, participant, sponsor, protocol, assessment, specimen, or study records without resolving FERPA, consent, Common Rule, sponsor terms, retention, access, data-use, or tenant policy constraints.

Suppressing conflicting records across research administration, IRB, sponsor portals, finance, SIS, LMS, document repositories, and department trackers instead of preserving each state and escalating.

Ignoring pending proposal routing, protocol amendment, continuing review, sponsor prior approval, FCOI review, no-cost extension, closeout, lab safety, or consent dependencies and presenting a workflow as ready.
Issuing ethics, academic, instructional, sponsor, grant, budget, audit, safety, accreditation, privacy, or legal decisions rather than assembling evidence and routing to the proper authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Institution-specific research administration workflows vary by sponsor, public/private status, state law, institutional official delegation, and tenant policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack requires tenant source hierarchy, sponsor terms, authority maps, and retrieval hooks at runtime; it blocks final outputs when those facts are missing or conflicting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: IRB applicability, exempt status, continuing review, consent validity, and participant-rights handling depend on study design, institutional policy, and authorized IRB determinations.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane escalates all IRB, consent validity, participant-rights, and ethics determinations; it only assembles administrative evidence, status, and owner routing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Grant allowability, sponsor prior approval, FCOI management adequacy, budget authority, and closeout acceptance are sponsor-specific and institution-specific.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses public federal baselines and official NIH/NSF policy where applicable, requires tenant award terms and grants authority, and refuses unsupported compliance or approval claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Retention schedules and access constraints vary by FERPA posture, Common Rule records, sponsor terms, state records law, data-use agreements, and tenant policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack requires tenant retention schedules and access policy at runtime and blocks deletion, publication, disclosure, transfer, or reclassification when rules conflict.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated specification-based package.
Tenant-specific source hierarchy, sponsor terms, system retrieval hooks, authority maps, IRB policy, and retention schedules remain required runtime prerequisites.
